explanations.2: This stores the Docker socket mount flag as a variable so you can reuse it in multiple commands without typing it repeatedly.
explanations.4: .PHONY tells Make that 'build' is not a file name but an action to always run, even if a file called 'build' exists.
explanations.10: The ${DOCKER_SOCK} variable gets replaced with the socket mount, and -v .:/app/src mounts your current folder into the container so your code changes are visible inside.
explanations.30: The --force flag removes the image even if containers are still using it, which is helpful for cleanup.
